title: Extension Registration and Route Mounting
activity_flow: START: Extension file loaded by host runtime
STEP 1: Extension calls extension.import('extensionController') → gets { ExtensionController, Controller, Get, Post, Put, Delete, HttpError }
STEP 2: Class is defined with @Controller(prefix, adminUsernames?, allowedAppIds?) decorator
STEP 3: Route methods are defined with @Get/@Post/@Put/@Delete(path, routeOptions?, adminUsernames?) decorators
→ each decorator uses addInitializer to push RouteMeta onto prototype.__routes[]
→ RouteMeta: { method, path, options, adminUsernames, allowedAppIds, handler }
LOOP for each route in prototype.__routes:
4b. Merge adminUsernames: controller-level UNION route-level (or whichever is defined)
4c. Merge allowedAppIds: controller-level UNION route-level (or whichever is defined)
→ this delegates to the Express router inside the host runtime
→ NO: throw HttpError(403, 'Only admins may request this resource.')
→ YES: CHECK req.actor.type.app.uid present AND not in allowedAppIds?
→ YES: throw HttpError(403, 'This app may not request this resource.')
5e (catch HttpError): build payload { error, code?, errorCode?, ...fields }, res.status(statusCode).send(payload), log warn
5f (catch Error): res.status(500).send({ error: message }), log error
5g (catch unknown): res.status(500).send({ error: 'An unknown error occurred' }), log error
key_conditions: Unsupported HTTP method → throw at registration time (stops loop)
No req.actor when auth required → 401 Unauthorized
App UID present but not in allowedAppIds → 403 Forbidden
HttpError thrown in handler → structured JSON error response with statusCode
decorator_metadata_flow: @Controller sets prefix/adminUsernames/allowedAppIds on prototype at class definition time
@Get/@Post/@Put/@Delete use TC39 stage-3 addInitializer to populate prototype.__routes[] at instance init time
registerRoutes() reads all metadata from Object.getPrototypeOf(this) at call time

key_types.RouteMeta: { method: HttpMethod, path: string, options?: EndpointOptions, handler: (req,res,next)=>void|Promise<void>, adminUsernames?: string[], allowedAppIds?: string[] }
key_types.EndpointOptions: { allowedMethods?, subdomain?, noauth?, mw?: RequestHandler[], otherOpts? }
key_types.extension[method]: (path: string, options: EndpointOptions, handler: RequestHandler) => void  (delegates to Express)

key_relationships: Controller/Get/Post/Put/Delete decorators write metadata to prototype (design-time coupling)
registerRoutes() reads prototype metadata and delegates to extension[method] (runtime coupling)
extension[method] delegates to Express Router (host runtime boundary)
important_notes: The global 'extension' object is provided by the host runtime (Puter backend). Extensions import the extensionController module via extension.import('extensionController'). Admin usernames always include 'admin' and 'system' as defaults when any admin restriction is set. Route guards are enforced in the wrapper closure, not in the handler itself.
